_meta.description: Persistent living knowledge for the Environmental Risks & Planetary Boundaries monitor (ERM). Carries forward planetary boundary statuses, tipping system flags, regulatory regimes, supply-chain choke points, climate-physics anchors, energy-transition stocks/flows, corporate disclosure, carbon markets, climate litigation, and physical risk events. Union of prior GERP state and 2026-04-28 DR baseline.
_meta.calibration: Copernicus 2025 + Planetary Boundaries 2023 applied 2026-04-02; WMO State of Global Climate 2024 (March 2025); NOAA Mauna Loa Feb 2026

planetary_boundary_status.key_indicator: 2024 global mean temp 1.55°C above 1850-1900 baseline — first full calendar year above 1.5°C; CO₂ at 429.35 ppm Feb 2026 (Mauna Loa)
planetary_boundary_status.data_source: WMO State of Global Climate 2024; NOAA GML Mauna Loa

planetary_boundary_status.week_development: Deforestation down 35% but rainfall decline 52-72% from deforestation

tipping_system_flags.proxy_metric: FovS freshwater flux at 34°S; western boundary overturning transport at 16.5°N–42.5°N
tipping_system_flags.proxy_value: Consistent linear decline in deep western overturning current across all measured latitudes; western boundary decline accounts for up to 90% of overall AMOC weakening
tipping_system_flags.collapse_probability_range: 34–62% before 2100 (Potsdam/NCAR/CSIRO ensemble, 2026); tipping threshold estimated at ~2.5°C warming above pre-industrial
tipping_system_flags.cascade_chain: AMOC slowdown -> Sahel drought -> food insecurity -> displacement -> conflict

tipping_system_flags.proxy_value: 1.4 km/year (IceSat-2 January 2026) — exceeds IPCC AR6 upper projection bound

tipping_system_flags.proxy_metric: Vegetation greenness anomaly and deforestation extent
tipping_system_flags.proxy_value: 14% below 2001–2020 baseline across 450,000 km²; ~19% total deforestation; 17-20% area past local tipping point (INPE 2024)
~280 Gt/year (2023–2025 average); 18 lowest Arctic sea-ice minimum extents all in past 18 years
tipping_system_flags.key_indicator: Stable loss; freshwater flux driving AMOC weakening
tipping_system_flags.source: Copernicus; WMO State of Global Climate 2024

tipping_system_flags.proxy_metric: Atmospheric CH₄ concentration and thermokarst expansion
tipping_system_flags.proxy_value: CH₄ at 1923 ppb; Siberian thermokarst expansion ongoing; carbon pool revised to 1,700 Gt C; permafrost thaw heating 3× faster than global average
tipping_system_flags.key_indicator: Stable thaw; third Arctic tipping at risk per WMO 2024
tipping_system_flags.source: PIK; Nature Climate Change 2024 Siberian thermokarst study

tipping_system_flags.proxy_metric: NOAA Coral Reef Watch bleaching alert level
tipping_system_flags.proxy_value: 4th global mass bleaching event confirmed April 2024; by 30 Sep 2025, ~84.4% of world coral reef area impacted; 83 countries affected. El Niño onset forecast April–June 2026 at 70% probability — fifth bleaching event risk rising

version_history.change: Initial baseline established at Critical — fourth global bleaching event confirmed

tipping_system_flags.key_indicator: 84.4% of reef area bleached; El Niño forecast 2026 raises risk of 5th event

tipping_system_flags.proxy_value: 4.28 million km² minimum in 2024 (7th lowest); all 18 lowest extents in past 18 years; Antarctic sea-ice minimum 2nd lowest in 2024 (3rd consecutive year below 2M km²)
tipping_system_flags.source: WMO State of Global Climate 2024; Copernicus

structural_facts.value: 1.55°C ± 0.13°C above 1850-1900 baseline (WMO synthesis); first full calendar year above 1.5°C; each of past 10 years individually the 10 warmest on record
429.35 ppm (Feb 2026, Mauna Loa); annual mean 427.35 ppm in 2025; peak 430.5 ppm May 2025 — highest in 800,000+ years
Each of past 8 years set new record for ocean heat content; 2024 record; sea-level rise rate has doubled since satellite measurements began
Largest three-year loss of glacier mass on record in past three years; exceptional negative mass balances in Norway, Sweden, Svalbard, tropical Andes

FovS weakening confirmed; 2026 Science Advances study (Xing et al.) shows consistent linear decline in deep western overturning at all latitudes; western boundary decline = 90% of total signal; AMOC tipping threshold estimated ~2.5°C warming; RAPID array: ~1 Sv/decade decline 2004-2023

Long-term warming currently 1.34–1.41°C (WMO range of methods); IPCC projects AMOC weakening 24–39% by 2100 depending on emissions scenario; Paris 1.5°C goal: first calendar year breach confirmed 2024

structural_facts.status: Definitive phase operational from 1 January 2026; first CBAM declaration and surrender due 30 September 2027 for 2026 imports; de minimis exemption (50 tonnes) under Omnibus I (Reg. EU 2025/2083, effective 20 Oct 2025); first official CBAM price published 7 April 2026 at €75.36/tCO₂

structural_facts.regime: CSRD — Corporate Sustainability Reporting Directive (Omnibus I revision)
structural_facts.status: Omnibus I finalised 24 February 2026; scope reduced ~90% — applies only to EU companies with >1,000 employees AND >€450M net turnover; second-wave reporting deferred from 2026 to 2028; listed SMEs deferred to 2029; first-wave (PIEs >1,000 employees) still reporting 2025

structural_facts.regime: CSDDD — Corporate Sustainability Due Diligence Directive (Omnibus I revision)
structural_facts.status: Scope narrowed: >5,000 employees AND >€1.5B net turnover; largest EU companies comply from 2028; transposition deadline July 2027; scope reduced ~70%

Package formally adopted Oct 2023; ETS2 (buildings, road transport) launching 2027; EUA price ~€75/tCO₂ as of April 2026 (near 2-year high); Market Stability Reserve emergency adjustment proposed April 2026; 8% supply cut scheduled 2026

OBBB (One Big Beautiful Bill) signed 4 July 2025: eliminates most IRA clean energy tax credits for projects not under construction within 60 days of enactment; full EV credit repeal; EV credits eliminated; EPA proposed rescission of 2009 GHG Endangerment Finding (Aug 2025); EPA final rule announced Feb 2026 repealing GHG vehicle standards — claimed $1.3T in savings; ExxonMobil challenging California climate disclosure laws (Oct 2025)

Labour government target: clean power meets 100% of demand by 2030 (95% low-carbon generation); NESO 2026 priorities confirm zero-carbon operability achieved Q1 2025 (97.7% zero-carbon for 30 min on 1 April 2025); CfD auctions 2025 and 2026 target offshore wind expansion to 27+ GW by 2030; strategic spatial energy plan to be delivered 2026

2035 NDC submitted Nov 2025: cut GHG emissions 7-10% from peak by 2035; ETS to expand beyond power sector to steel, cement, aluminium, chemicals by 2027; absolute carbon caps introduced; target carbon-neutral before 2060; export controls on 12 rare earth elements (April 2025, effective Dec 2025 — 90% of separation capacity)

Cabinet approved March 2026: 47% emissions intensity reduction from 2005 levels by 2035; 60% non-fossil installed capacity by 2035 (target already 52.57% achieved Feb 2026 — 5 years ahead); carbon sink 3.5-4 Gt CO₂e; net-zero by 2070

UNFCCC COP29 (Baku, Nov 2024): $300B/year by 2035 to developing countries; $1.3T/year mobilisation goal (Baku-to-Belem Roadmap); Article 6 carbon market rules finalised; Loss and Damage Fund: $431M pledged total, Barbados Implementation Modalities $250M pilot launched Dec 2025

COP30 held Belem, Brazil, 10-21 November 2025; NDC update cycle; Tropical Forest Forever Facility (TFFF) launched targeting $125B; Loss and Damage Fund pilot active; Baku-to-Belem Roadmap in progress; six-axis Action Agenda; Article 6 implementation

structural_facts.price_current: ~€75/tCO₂ (April 2026); year-end 2025 at +16% YTD at ~€80/t; first CBAM price €75.36/tCO₂ published 7 April 2026
structural_facts.price_trajectory: Forecasts range €70-€160/t by 2030 depending on regulatory review outcome; 8% supply cut in 2026; ETS2 launching 2027
structural_facts.governance_status: Market Stability Reserve emergency adjustment proposed April 2026; wider ETS review expected Q3 2026

structural_facts.status: Article 6 rules finalised at COP29 (Nov 2024); Article 6.4 credits (A6.4ERs) not expected until 2026 at earliest; CDM transition projects may issue 2025; bilateral Article 6.2 deals proliferating
ICVCM CCP Impact Report 2025 shows market reshaping around Core Carbon Principles; increased focus on integrity, credit quality, transparency; assessment programme for CCP approval ongoing

Effective for annual reporting periods from 1 January 2024; April 2025 consultation on IFRS S2 amendments (simplify Scope 3 Financed Emissions reporting); Category 15 financed emissions guidance published May 2025; adoption accelerating across jurisdictions
structural_facts.jurisdictions_adopted: Australia, Canada, Japan, Singapore, UK (aligned), Brazil (mandatory from 2026)

structural_facts.status: 10,000 company validations reached January 2026; 11,000 committed or validated by mid-2025; covers >40% of global market cap; Corporate Net-Zero Standard v2.0 second draft published November 2025 — requires board-level oversight, transition plans within 12 months, distinct Scope 1/2 targets, 100% low-carbon electricity by 2040

structural_facts.framework: TNFD — Taskforce on Nature-related Financial Disclosures
structural_facts.status: 620 organisations (>USD 20T AUM, >USD 7T market cap) committed to TNFD-aligned disclosures as of July 2025 (TNFD Status Report Sep 2025); 89% of respondents reported or plan to report; 77% want ISSB to adopt TNFD into global baseline; regulatory mandates approaching in Europe and Asia; data gaps in biodiversity measurement remain

structural_facts.case: ICJ Advisory Opinion on Climate Change
structural_facts.status: ISSUED — 23 July 2025; unanimous opinion: states have binding obligations under international law to protect climate for present and future generations; 1.5°C is legally pivotal; fossil fuel licensing, production, subsidising can constitute customary international law breach; sets precedent for state responsibility, NDC progression requirements, reparations for vulnerable countries
structural_facts.significance: Landmark — authoritative reference for litigation globally; ICJ affirmed NDC progression is legally required; supplements ITLOS Advisory Opinion (May 2024)

structural_facts.status: ISSUED — July 2025; declared climate change a human rights emergency; recognised right to healthy climate; states must prevent and mitigate climate harms
structural_facts.significance: Regional precedent for Latin American and Caribbean jurisdictions; feeds into COP30 Brazil context

structural_facts.case: Suncor Energy v. Boulder County (US Supreme Court)
structural_facts.status: PENDING — US Supreme Court agreed to hear case (Feb 2026); Colorado Supreme Court ruled May 2025 that federal law does not preempt state climate tort claims; SCOTUS ruling expected by mid-2027; outcome will determine whether ~30+ similar state/municipal climate accountability suits may proceed
structural_facts.significance: Potentially forecloses or opens entire category of state-law climate damages suits in US

structural_facts.case: French court — major energy company greenwashing
structural_facts.status: RULED — October 23, 2025; French civil court held that carbon neutrality 2050 claims were misleading; company ordered to remove claims within 1 month; financial penalties for non-compliance; sets EU greenwashing litigation precedent under consumer protection law
structural_facts.significance: First European consumer-law ruling on corporate net-zero claims; precedent for CSRD enforcement

structural_facts.case: US state attorney general anti-ESG campaign
structural_facts.status: ACTIVE — Sep 2025: 16 Republican AGs sent letters to major tech companies questioning renewable energy claim accuracy (unbundled RECs); ExxonMobil filed federal challenge to California SB 253/261 climate disclosure laws Oct 2025; Supreme Court emergency injunction application filed Nov 2025
structural_facts.significance: Bidirectional litigation risk: climate accountability AND anti-ESG pushback; regulatory fragmentation risk for corporate disclosure

structural_facts.status: Published 2025: rapid growth in climate litigation; expanding to financial institutions, directors & officers; D&O cases on fiduciary failure; Polish Enea case against former directors for approving coal plant (pending); focus on consumer/investor protection, greenwashing, ESG investing
structural_facts.significance: Fourth in series; documents systemic expansion of litigation targets

windowed_events.economic_impact: USD 61.2B in damages — costliest wildfire on record; $250-275B total AccuWeather estimate including economic loss
windowed_events.significance: Costliest wildfire in US history; approximately twice previous record; climate change attribution

windowed_events.economic_impact: 23 events, $115B total damages, 276 fatalities; third-highest annual count after 2023 (28) and 2024 (27); average gap between events: 10 days
windowed_events.significance: Structural acceleration: 1980s average was 3 events/year; 2016-2025 average is 20 events/year

windowed_events.economic_impact: USD 220B global economic losses (Swiss Re preliminary); $107B insured losses; down 33% from 2024 ($327B) partly due to no US hurricane landfall
windowed_events.significance: 2025 third-most expensive year for extreme weather globally despite no major US hurricane

windowed_events.economic_impact: USD 79.6B; 219 deaths; Category 4 landfall near Perry, Florida; historic flooding in Appalachia; NOAA billion-dollar disaster #1 of 2024
windowed_events.significance: Strongest hurricane on record to strike Big Bend, Florida; rainfall and flooding exceeded 1916 records in western NC

windowed_events.economic_impact: 27 events, $182.7B damages, 568 fatalities; 4th-costliest year on record
Effective Dec 2025: licensing required for 12 rare earth elements; indirect supply chain impacts modelled at 5-10× direct cost increases
windowed_events.status: Active (suspended for US only under 12-month US-China trade agreement)
windowed_events.significance: China controls ~90% of global rare earth separation capacity; strategic vulnerability for EVs, wind turbines, defence, semiconductors

windowed_events.economic_impact: Structural removal of regulatory basis for GHG vehicle standards; $1.3T claimed regulatory savings; downstream effect on clean energy investment certainty
windowed_events.significance: Most consequential US climate regulatory rollback; legal challenges expected; post-Loper Bright framing

windowed_events.event: 4th Global Coral Bleaching Event — ongoing through Sep 2025
windowed_events.economic_impact: 84.4% of world coral reef area impacted; 83 countries affected; fourth global event confirmed April 2024
windowed_events.status: Concluded Sep 2025; 5th event risk rising with El Niño forecast April-June 2026
windowed_events.significance: Longest and most extensive bleaching event on record; baseline reef health permanently shifted

windowed_events.value: IEA Renewables 2025: 685 GW installed in 2024 (+22% YoY); >750 GW expected 2025; solar PV accounts for ~80% of additions (~600 GW in 2025); solar PV contributed >25% of total global energy demand growth in 2025 — first time any source has led; renewables projected to double by 2030 to 4.6 TW cumulative additions

106 GW new capacity globally in 2025 (+43% YoY); total cumulative ~270 GW/630 GWh; 2026 forecast >450 GWh new. US: 28 GW/57.6 GWh installed in 2025 (+29% YoY); US cumulative 137 GWh utility-scale + 19 GWh C&I + 9 GWh residential
20.7 million EVs sold globally in 2025 (+20% YoY); China: 12.9M (+17%); Europe: 4.3M (+33%); EVs account for 1-in-4 new vehicles in 2025; global EV fleet ~58M end-2024; 2026 forecast 23M (+15.7%)

Hydrogen Council (Sep 2025): >USD 110B committed across 500+ projects past FID; 6 million mt/year low-carbon hydrogen capacity; China leads with $33B (55% of capacity, 19 GW); North America $23B focused on CCS-enabled H2; CCS in Europe: Stockholm Exergi BECCS, Oslo Klemetsrud, Project Greensand Denmark, Fluxys Belgium all reached FID 2024-2025

GB achieved zero-carbon operation for 30 mins on 1 Apr 2025 (97.7%); NESO 2026 goal: make zero-carbon operation routine; CfD auctions 2025 and 2026 critical; 80 new transmission projects needed by 2030

structural_facts.choke_point: China rare earth / critical minerals processing
structural_facts.dominance_share: ~90% of global rare earth separation capacity; 80% of permanent magnet production; 90% of heavy rare earth processing; 95% of some elements
structural_facts.dependency_structure: US imports 70% of rare earth materials from China; 12 REE elements under export licensing from Dec 2025 (effective under Regulations on Rare Earth Administration). US-China trade agreement May 2026 suspends controls for 12 months for US. Industries exposed: EV ($260B+), defence ($800B+), semiconductors ($630B+), renewables ($150B+)

windowed_events.event: China imposes rare earth export licensing (April 2025)
windowed_events.detail: First major restriction since 2010; effective Dec 2025; 12 elements including holmium, erbium, europium

US-China trade deal: general licences for rare earths, gallium, germanium, antimony, graphite; controls suspended for US for 12 months; AUS-US $8.5B partnership to reduce Chinese dependence by 25% by 2030

structural_facts.dominance_share: ~50% of global nickel reserves; largest global nickel producer; dominant in HPAL processing for EV-grade nickel
structural_facts.dependency_structure: Critical for NMC/NCA EV batteries; Chinese companies operate ~70% of Indonesian smelting capacity; environmental degradation concerns in Sulawesi
Critical for EV batteries; ASM sector high proportion; child/forced labour ESG risk; Chinese companies control ~80% of Congolese cobalt output; supply constraints ongoing amid conflict

structural_facts.dominance_share: Ukraine + Russia = ~30% of global wheat exports; ~65% of sunflower oil
structural_facts.dependency_structure: Ukraine-Russia war disruption ongoing; UN-brokered grain deal lapsed July 2023; alternative routing via Romania/Danube established; price volatility in MENA/Sub-Saharan Africa food importers

structural_facts.dominance_share: ~30% of global trade; 80,000 vessels/year; 25% of global oil flows
structural_facts.dependency_structure: Critical chokepoint for LNG, crude oil, container shipping Asia-Europe; piracy risk minimal but concentrated; Singapore-Malaysia-Indonesia coastal states; alternative: Lombok/Sunda Straits (deep-draft vessels)

structural_facts.dominance_share: Semiconductor fab (Taiwan/Arizona water stress); lithium brine (Atacama freshwater stress); coal/thermal power (India/China Yangtze Basin drought vulnerability)
structural_facts.dependency_structure: Taiwan TSMC water recycling >85%; Arizona fab growth stressing Colorado River basin; Atacama lithium extraction conflicts with indigenous water rights; GRACE satellite data shows accelerating groundwater depletion in major agricultural zones (North India, MENA)

structural_facts.status: Adopted Dec 2022; 23 targets for 2030, including 30×30 (protect 30% of land and ocean by 2030); implementation ongoing; NBSAPs being revised; capacity-building workshops East/Southeast Asia June 2025; financing gap remains significant

620 organisations committed as of July 2025 (>$20T AUM); 1,800 Forum members; average 8.7/14 TNFD recommended disclosures reported; 89% reported or plan to report nature-related info; 91% want ISSB to incorporate TNFD; regulatory mandates imminent in Europe and Asia

loss_damage_finance.committed: USD 431.14 million total pledges to Fund for Responding to Loss and Damage
loss_damage_finance.disbursed: USD 0 (Barbados Implementation Modalities pilot opened Dec 2025; $250M allocated for 2025-2026 grants; first proposals due Dec 2025-Jun 2026)

version_history.change: Initial baseline established — $0 disbursed of $661M committed

active_attribution_gap_cases.description: 140,000+ synthetic chemicals released; <3,000 tested for toxicity. No binding international governance framework.
active_attribution_gap_cases.key_metric: 140000 plus synthetic chemicals, less than 3000 tested

active_attribution_gap_cases.description: ISA failed to finalise exploitation regulations by 2023 deadline. Applications in review. No baseline monitoring of benthic ecosystems prior to extraction.

active_attribution_gap_cases.case: Atmospheric Aerosol Loading — Shipping Fuel Transition
active_attribution_gap_cases.description: IMO 2020 sulphur cap reducing aerosol masking effect; no liability mechanism for accelerated warming attributable to aerosol reduction.

active_attribution_gap_cases.case: Coral Reef Bleaching — No International Liability Framework
active_attribution_gap_cases.description: Fourth global mass bleaching event in progress (concluded Sep 2025; fifth event risk rising). No binding international liability framework for bleaching losses attributable to state emissions.
active_attribution_gap_cases.governance_status: No binding framework; ICJ AO July 2025 may open reparations pathway
active_attribution_gap_cases.key_metric: 84.4% of global reef area affected; El Niño returning 2026

active_attribution_gap_cases.case: Atmospheric Aerosol Loading -- Shipping Fuel Transition
Coral Reef Bleaching -- No International Liability Framework

regional_cascade_chains.chain: Freshwater depletion → food insecurity → displacement → political instability
regional_cascade_chains.reverse_cascade: Armed conflict in Sudan/Mali blocking adaptation investment — confirmed

version_history.reason: Inaugural issue; FEWS NET Q1 2026 data

regional_cascade_chains.chain: Glacial retreat → seasonal water stress → agricultural productivity loss → political instability
regional_cascade_chains.reverse_cascade: India–Pakistan nuclear posture increasing Himalayan wildfire/disruption risk — assessed moderate

regional_cascade_chains.chain: Sea ice loss → new maritime access → resource competition → governance vacuum → geopolitical escalation
regional_cascade_chains.reverse_cascade: Russian Arctic resource extraction directly accelerating climate forcing — confirmed

planetary_boundaries_calibration.source: Richardson et al. (2023) Science Advances + Copernicus C3S 2025

climate_change.gerp_note: Baseline; track departure from trend only

land_system_change.gerp_note: Track FAO Global Forest Resources Assessment

atmospheric_aerosol_loading.gerp_note: Track IMO 2020 sulphur cap second-order effects

copernicus_2025_baselines.source: Copernicus C3S European State of the Climate 2025; WMO State of Global Climate 2024 (March 2025)
copernicus_2025_baselines.gerp_note: First full calendar year above 1.5°C confirmed (2024 = 1.55°C). WMO long-term trend still 1.34-1.41°C. Next threshold to watch: first 5-year rolling average above 1.5°C.

amoc.source: Boers (2021) + 2024 replication studies; Xing et al. 2026 Science Advances — western boundary decline confirms systemic trend

amazon.gerp_note: Track PRODES/INPE deforestation quarterly; F3 signal if year-on-year acceleration confirmed

coral_reefs.gerp_note: Fourth bleaching event concluded Sep 2025 after impacting 84.4% of reef area. El Niño returning April-June 2026 — fifth event risk elevated. Baseline for reef health has shifted permanently downward.

permafrost.source: Nature Climate Change 2024 Siberian thermokarst study

calibration_log.source: Copernicus C3S 2025 + Richardson et al. 2023 + INPE 2024
calibration_log.summary: Planetary boundaries status table, 1.5C baseline shift, AMOC FovS thresholds, Amazon partial tipping, coral reef event 4, permafrost methane underestimate, attribution gap flags

calibration_log.source: WMO State of Global Climate 2024 (Mar 2025); NOAA GML Mauna Loa Feb 2026; EU CBAM/CSRD Omnibus I; US EPA deregulation; COP29/30 outcomes; ICJ AO July 2025; NOAA CRW 2025; IEA Renewables 2025; Hydrogen Council Sep 2025; TNFD Status Report Sep 2025; SBTi Trend Tracker 2025; Climate Central 2025 disaster count; Swiss Re 2025
calibration_log.summary: Full DR baseline: climate physics anchors (2024 first year >1.5°C, CO2 429 ppm), regulatory regimes (CBAM live, CSRD Omnibus narrowed 90%, US IRA gutted, India NDC updated, China ETS expansion, COP30 outcomes), carbon markets (EU ETS ~€75, Article 6 finalised, CBAM first price), disclosure (ISSB S1/S2, SBTi 10K validations, TNFD 620 adopters), litigation (ICJ AO issued, SCOTUS Boulder case, French greenwashing ruling), physical events (LA wildfires $61B, 84.4% reef area bleached), energy transition (685 GW renewables 2024, 20.7M EVs 2025, 106 GW BESS), supply chain choke points with dominance shares, nature/biodiversity (CBD GBF, TNFD), new structural modules added

gaps_register.finding: Physical asset positions per G20 jurisdiction (spec dimension 1) — pipelines, ports, water rights, transmission corridors, data centre water dependencies, semiconductor fab exposure — not populated from this DR run; requires dedicated per-jurisdiction asset-level research
gaps_register.suggested_action: Operator-directed follow-up research per G20 jurisdiction; consider using IEA World Energy Investment and infrastructure databases as primary sources

gaps_register.finding: EU Methane Regulation status (specifically Oil & Gas Methane Regulation 2024/1787) not captured in detail; monitoring and repair requirements entering force
gaps_register.suggested_action: Add EU Methane Regulation entry to regulatory_regimes in next verification

gaps_register.finding: G20 country coverage for windowed events incomplete — spec stop condition requires ≥3 windowed events per G20 jurisdiction; Brazil, Japan, Germany, France, Australia, South Africa, Saudi Arabia, Turkey, Mexico, South Korea, Canada events not individually enumerated
gaps_register.suggested_action: Supplement with per-country event research in next run; IEA, NOAA, Munich Re Nat-Cat data

gaps_register.finding: Climate physics anchors below 30-entry cap — only 6 structural facts populated; additional anchors available (sea level rise rate, ocean de-oxygenation, methane atmospheric burden, attribution science for specific event classes) but deliberately capped per spec
gaps_register.suggested_action: Up to 24 additional anchors could be added to reach spec cap of 30 without scope creep; suggest batch additions in next quarterly refresh
